home.subtitle: A sacred abode where divine blessings flow eternally. Experience the spiritual heritage and timeless traditions of this ancient temple.
home.aboutPreview: Sri Kanukondaraya Swamy Devasthanam stands as a beacon of devotion and spiritual heritage in Chittoor District, Andhra Pradesh. With centuries of sacred traditions, this temple continues to be a pilgrimage destination for thousands of devotees seeking divine blessings.
home.heroBadge: A Manifestation of Lord Venkateswara Swamy · Since 1,000+ Years
home.heroTagline: Where the Lord Himself walks among His people — not in a statue, but in a living, breathing Sacred Ox. Witness a thousand-year tradition where the Divine chooses humility over grandeur.
home.reconstructionText: The temple is being rebuilt from scratch — a ₹7 Crore government project on 2 acres. Grand festival celebrations are temporarily suspended.
home.identitySubtitle: Unlike any other temple in India — here, Lord Vishnu does not reside in stone. He walks among His people as a living, breathing Sacred Ox.
home.identityCard1Body: In most temples, the Ox is Nandi — Lord Shiva's vehicle. Here, the Devara Eddu is a direct living manifestation (Vibhavarupa) of Lord Maha Vishnu himself. A truth preserved for over 1,000 years.
home.identityCard2Body: While other shrines house statues and symbols, Lord Kanukondaraya manifests in a physical, breathing form — surveying His lands and protecting His people, just as He did during the Great Drought.
home.identityCard3Body: Where Vaishnavism and Shaivism unite in harmony. The soul of Lord Vishnu fills a form associated with Shiva's tradition. Maha Shivaratri is celebrated with great grandeur here.
home.identityLink: Learn the full story of the Devara Eddu
home.stepsSubtitle: During the Great Drought of the 19th Century, Lord Venkateswara manifested as the Sacred Ox and took three miraculous steps across Chittoor to lead His people to safety and water.

about.title: About the Temple
about.historyContent:
Sri Kanukondaraya Swamy Devasthanam is an ancient temple situated in Pedakanti Palli (Village), Gangadhara Nellore Mandal, Chittoor District, Andhra Pradesh. The temple, dedicated to Sri Kanukondaraya Swamy, has been a centre of spiritual worship for centuries. The temple architecture reflects the Dravidian style with intricate carvings and a majestic gopuram that stands as a testament to the rich cultural heritage of the region.

The temple was established during the period of the great Vijayanagara Empire and has since been a pivotal spiritual centre. Renovated and expanded over the centuries by devoted kings and philanthropists, the temple today stands as a beautiful amalgamation of ancient tradition and timeless devotion.

Devotees from across Andhra Pradesh and neighbouring states visit this sacred temple to seek the blessings of Sri Kanukondaraya Swamy, particularly during the annual Brahmotsavam which draws thousands of pilgrims.
about.deityContent:
Lord Raya Swami, the presiding deity of this temple, is worshipped as a powerful manifestation of divine grace. The deity is believed to bestow prosperity, good health, and spiritual upliftment upon devotees who visit with sincere devotion.

The main idol is an awe-inspiring sculpted form, adorned with precious ornaments and garlands of fresh flowers daily. The sanctum sanctorum radiates an atmosphere of profound peace and divine energy that touches every visitor's heart.

Special significance is attached to the Friday and Saturday pujas, where the deity is adorned with special alankaram and offered elaborate naivedyam prepared with traditional recipes passed down through generations.

threeSteps.heroSubtitle: When the Great Drought threatened His people, Lord Venkateswara manifested as a Sacred Ox and walked across the land — His three footprints marking the path from despair to divine abundance.
threeSteps.step1Text: The first miraculous step — the Devara Eddu signaled its presence here, marking the beginning of the exodus from the drought-stricken north. The first spark of hope.
threeSteps.step2Text: The Ox took a miraculous leap from the plains directly to the hilltop — an impossible feat proving its divine, supernatural nature.
threeSteps.step3Text: The final step — as the footprint touched earth here, the drought broke and the land was blessed with water and abundance. The Lord settled here permanently.
threeSteps.ctaTitle: Walk in the Footsteps of the Divine
threeSteps.ctaSubtitle: Visit Pedakanti Palli — the Chosen Land where the Sacred Ox rests
